Action: To prepare to do this test make sure there are 3 journals publically available in DOAJ one with discontinued date in the past one with discontinued date in the future one with discontinued date today
Search for every journal from the list above

Expected Test Results: On the ToC of the journal with discontinued date in the past or today - the discontinued date is displayed
On the ToC of the journal with discontinued date in the future - the discontinued date is not displayed

Action: To prepare for this test, ensure that a journal is publicly available in DOAJ, containing a minimum of 4 articles, with 2 from one month and 2 from another, and these months must not be consecutive (e.g. 2 articles from January and 2 from March).

Expected Test Results: On the "Articles" tab in the ToC of the journal, the articles are displayed.
On the left, the "Month" facet is displayed.

Action: Select one of the years from the Year facet
Select one of the months from the list

Expected Test Results: On the "Articles" tab in the ToC of the journal, the articles from the selected month are displayed.
On the left, the "Month" facet is displayed.
The selected month is displayed as selected in the "Month" facet.

Action: ## Journal with Last Full Review date

To prepare for this test, ensure that a journal is publicly available in DOAJ with a Last Full Review date set in the past. You can use the testdrive setup linked here if none are available

http://testdoaj.cottagelabs.com//testdrive/publisher_with_journal

Search for the above journal on the public search page (e.g. by the identifier provided by the testdrive)

http://testdoaj.cottagelabs.com//search/journals
click the journal title to go to the ToC page

Expected Test Results: At the bottom of the ToC page is a "Last Full Review" date, showing the date set in the journal record.
